content_promise: Turn one idea into a clear, swipeable lesson.

slides.headline: Your hook goes here
slides.body: Use one short promise or tension line.
slides.creator_note: Make the promise specific to the reader.
slides.alt_text: Opening slide with a large headline introducing the carousel promise.

slides.headline: Name the problem
slides.body: Explain why the reader should care.
slides.visual_direction: Simple problem statement with one icon or contrast block.
slides.creator_note: Keep this grounded in the source material.
slides.alt_text: Slide explaining the reader problem and why it matters.

slides.headline: Show the better lens
slides.body: Turn the problem into a new way of thinking.
slides.creator_note: Make the reframe memorable and simple.
slides.alt_text: Slide contrasting the old way with a better perspective.

slides.headline: Step one
slides.visual_direction: Numbered step with one supporting symbol.
slides.alt_text: Slide presenting the first action step.

slides.headline: Step two
slides.visual_direction: Numbered step with a short example.
slides.creator_note: Connect this to the previous slide.
slides.alt_text: Slide presenting the second action step.

slides.headline: Step three
slides.visual_direction: Numbered step with a simple checklist item.
slides.alt_text: Slide presenting the third action step.

slides.headline: The quick recap
slides.body: Summarize the key lesson in three short bullets.
slides.alt_text: Slide summarizing the carousel's key lessons.

slides.headline: Try this next
slides.body: Invite one clear action that matches the goal.
slides.visual_direction: Simple final prompt with room for a CTA.
slides.creator_note: Avoid asking for too many actions.
slides.alt_text: Final slide inviting the reader to take one next step.

caption: Add context that does not simply repeat the slides.
